description: Suggestion rules: first matching category wins (except critical is always keep). Prefixes match package name start.

categories.label: Critical (Samsung / telephony)
categories.reason: Removing can break calls, mobile data, IMS, or home screen. Only change with a known replacement.

categories.label: Critical (OnePlus / OxygenOS)
categories.reason: Core OnePlus system components — removing can break launcher, calls, or camera.

categories.label: Critical (Google Pixel)
categories.reason: Core Pixel system components — removing can break launcher, calls, or setup.

categories.label: Social — Meta / Facebook
categories.reason: Facebook / Meta preinstall stubs; often removed on debloat lists if unused.

categories.label: Social — Netflix / streaming stubs
categories.reason: Pre-installed streaming stubs; safe to remove if you don't use these services.

categories.label: Samsung Galaxy Store
categories.reason: App store; removing blocks Galaxy Store installs/updates for Samsung apps.

categories.label: Browser-related (Samsung + Chrome)
categories.reason: Browsers and browser stubs; safe to remove if you use another browser or want to restrict web access.

categories.label: Search / Finder (can open web)
categories.reason: Galaxy Finder or search widgets can open web results; some parents remove to restrict web access.

categories.label: Microsoft (Phone Link / Office / OneDrive)
categories.reason: Link to Windows / Office / OneDrive stubs; optional on phones.

categories.label: Samsung Game features
categories.reason: Game Launcher / tools / optimization; remove if you do not use Samsung gaming features.

categories.label: Google Play Games
categories.reason: Google Play Games service; safe to remove if you don't play games.

categories.label: OnePlus Game Space
categories.reason: OnePlus gaming overlay and installer; safe to remove if you don't game.

categories.label: AR / VR (Samsung)
categories.reason: AR Zone, AR Doodle, AR Emoji, Gear VR service, stickers — optional.

categories.label: AR Core (Google)
categories.reason: Google AR framework; some apps (Maps live view, Measure) depend on it. Remove if you don't use AR features.

categories.label: Bixby (Samsung)
categories.reason: Bixby voice/UI pieces; some removals may need testing on your One UI version.

categories.label: Samsung apps (optional)
categories.reason: Samsung pre-installed apps that are safe to remove; do not affect core phone functionality.

categories.label: ANT+ (fitness radios)
categories.reason: Remove only if you do not use ANT+ accessories (some Garmin / bike sensors).

categories.label: Carrier bloatware (Verizon / AT&T / T-Mobile)
categories.reason: Carrier-installed apps; safe to remove unless you use the carrier's specific features.

categories.label: OnePlus apps (optional)
categories.reason: OnePlus pre-installed apps that are safe to remove; do not affect core phone functionality.

categories.label: OnePlus Zen / Focus Mode
categories.reason: Zen Mode and Work Life Balance; useful features for some. Remove only if unused.

categories.label: HeyTap / OPLUS cloud services
categories.reason: OPPO/OnePlus cloud sync and account services; safe to remove if you don't use HeyTap/OPLUS cloud.

categories.label: Pixel apps (optional)
categories.reason: Pixel-specific extras; safe to remove but some users find them useful.

categories.label: Google entertainment (Music / TV / Books)
categories.reason: Google entertainment apps; safe to remove and can be reinstalled from Play Store.

categories.label: Google apps (optional)
categories.reason: Often kept for Gmail, Maps, Drive. Remove only if you use alternatives.

categories.label: Google Assistant
categories.reason: Google voice assistant; safe to disable but some users rely on it for hands-free features.

categories.label: Google Play Store
categories.reason: Removing blocks normal Play installs/updates; use Family Link to restrict instead.

categories.label: Parental / Family Link
categories.reason: Keep if you use Google Family Link on this device.

categories.label: Samsung Account
categories.reason: Deeply integrated into Samsung phones. Removing can cause persistent error popups, break Find My Mobile, Samsung Cloud, and Settings pages that reference your Samsung account.

categories.label: Google Text-to-Speech
categories.reason: Used by Google Maps for turn-by-turn voice navigation, accessibility screen readers, and any app that reads text aloud. Removing breaks spoken directions and TTS features.

categories.label: Android OS Framework
categories.reason: Core AOSP internals: system providers, navigation overlays, display cutouts, backup transports, and essential services. Removing these can break system stability, navigation, or backup functionality.

categories.label: Android OS Optional (screensavers, live wallpaper, easter egg, printing)
categories.reason: Non-critical AOSP extras: screensavers (Daydream), live wallpaper picker, easter egg, and print spooler. Safe to remove if unused, but will not free much space.

categories.label: Google System Services
categories.reason: Google infrastructure overlays, config providers, sync adapters, and module metadata. These support GMS, cell broadcast, permissions, and system updates. Removing can break Google services or OTA updates.

categories.label: Samsung System Framework
categories.reason: Core Samsung services including providers, authentication framework, locale and timezone management, connectivity overlays, and network stack. Removing can break Samsung system functionality, contacts sync, or Wi-Fi.

categories.label: Samsung Telephony / Carrier
categories.reason: Samsung and carrier telephony components: IMS, RIL, ePDG, visual voicemail, Wi-Fi calling, and carrier provisioning. Removing can break calls, texts, or mobile data.

categories.label: Samsung Security / Biometrics
categories.reason: Knox core security, biometric authentication (face, fingerprint), Samsung Pass, Secure Folder, Find My Mobile, and KG client. Removing can break device security, lock screen, or remote-find features.

categories.label: Hardware / Vendor Drivers
categories.reason: Qualcomm GPU drivers, secure UI, time service, and Samsung GPU/Vulkan layers. These are required for hardware acceleration and secure display. Removing can cause graphical glitches or boot issues.

categories.label: Factory / Diagnostic / Test
categories.reason: Factory test apps, hidden menus, diagnostic agents, and service mode tools. These are used for hardware diagnostics and carrier testing. Harmless to keep; removing may prevent warranty service diagnostics.

categories.label: Samsung Emergency / Safety
categories.reason: Emergency mode, emergency launcher, SOS messaging, and safety information. These features can be life-saving in emergencies. Removing may prevent access to emergency services.

categories.label: Setup Wizards / Fonts / Auto-Install Config
categories.reason: Setup wizards handle initial device configuration and Google account linking. Fonts provide system-wide typeface support. Removing setup wizards can prevent factory reset recovery; removing fonts may cause display issues.

categories.label: Samsung Accessibility Features
categories.reason: Accessibility services including TalkBack (screen reader), hearing adjustment, one-hand mode, magnifier, and easy mode contacts. These are essential for users with disabilities. Safe to keep even if unused.

categories.label: Samsung Camera / Media
categories.reason: Samsung camera SDK, gallery, video player, photo editor, video trimmer, and related media services. Safe to remove if you use alternative camera/gallery apps, but removing the main camera app will prevent taking photos.

categories.label: Samsung DeX / Desktop Mode
categories.reason: Samsung DeX desktop experience: DeX on PC, desktop launcher, desktop SystemUI, and MDX framework. Safe to remove if you never connect your phone to a monitor or PC for desktop mode.

categories.label: Samsung Smart Switch
categories.reason: Smart Switch and EasyMover help transfer data between phones. Safe to remove after initial setup, but useful to keep if you plan to migrate to a new device later.

categories.label: Samsung Pay / Billing
categories.reason: Samsung Pay mobile payment and Samsung billing for in-app purchases. Safe to remove if you use Google Pay or don't make Samsung app purchases.

categories.label: Samsung Edge UI / Panels
categories.reason: Edge panels (apps, tasks, clipboard), Always-On Display service, dynamic lock screen, smart widgets, smart capture (screenshot tools), and people stripe. Safe to remove if you don't use Edge panel or AOD features.

categories.label: Samsung Sharing / Connectivity
categories.reason: Smart View mirroring, AllShare file/media sharing, Link Sharing, ShareLive, and Private Share. Safe to remove if you don't cast your screen or share files between Samsung devices.

categories.label: Samsung Productivity Apps
categories.reason: Samsung's built-in productivity apps: Contacts, Calendar, Email, My Files, Clock, Reminder, Keyboard (Honeyboard), voice input, handwriting, dictionary, and sound tools. Safe to remove if you use Google or third-party alternatives, but ensure you have a replacement keyboard before removing Honeyboard.

categories.label: Samsung Device Care / Account
categories.reason: Samsung Device Care (battery/storage optimization), Samsung Account sign-in, cloud services, AI recommendations (Rubin), analytics, app update center, and FOTA agent. Removing may prevent Samsung account features, OTA updates through Samsung channels, or device optimization tools.

categories.label: Samsung Themes / Wallpaper
categories.reason: Theme center, wallpaper resources, multi-wallpaper pack, dynamic lock screen wallpapers, Dress Room (AR outfit try-on), and personalization service. Safe to remove if you don't customize themes or wallpapers through Samsung's tools.

categories.label: Samsung Knox Enterprise
categories.reason: Knox enterprise management: analytics, push manager, KPE core, KLMS agent, Knox VPN proxy, MDM, cloud MDM, and SIM pin MDM. Safe to remove on personal devices not managed by an organization. Required for work profiles and enterprise management.

categories.label: Samsung Link to Windows
categories.reason: Samsung's Link to Windows service for phone-to-PC connectivity. Separate from DeX desktop mode. Safe to remove if you don't use the Windows Your Phone / Phone Link app.

categories.label: Samsung Kids / Digital Wellbeing
categories.reason: Kids installer sets up Samsung Kids mode. Digital Wellbeing (forest) tracks screen time and app usage. Safe to remove if you don't use parental controls or screen time tracking through Samsung.

categories.label: Samsung Wearable / Covers
categories.reason: Watch manager stub for Galaxy Watch pairing, and LED/back cover accessories. Safe to remove if you don't own a Galaxy Watch or LED cover case.

categories.label: Samsung Blockchain
categories.reason: Samsung Blockchain cold wallet service for cryptocurrency key storage. Safe to remove if you don't use Samsung's blockchain wallet feature.

categories.label: Samsung Weather
categories.reason: Samsung's built-in weather app. Safe to remove if you use a third-party weather app.

categories.label: Caller ID / Spam Protection (Hiya)
categories.reason: Hiya caller ID and spam call protection, often pre-installed on Samsung devices. Safe to remove if you use a different caller ID app or don't want spam filtering.

categories.label: Google Optional Apps
categories.reason: Optional Google apps: Android Auto, Google TTS, Files/Documents UI, Device Health (Turbo), Private Compute Services, Feedback, Location History, Carrier WiFi, Restore, and print recommendation. Safe to remove based on personal usage, but Google TTS is needed for text-to-speech and Restore helps with device migration.

categories.label: Samsung SmartThings
categories.reason: Samsung SmartThings IoT platform service for controlling smart home devices. Safe to remove if you don't use SmartThings-compatible smart home devices.

categories.label: Samsung Wi-Fi Security (WIPS)
categories.reason: Samsung Wi-Fi Intrusion Prevention System (MobileWIPS) monitors for rogue access points and Wi-Fi attacks. Part of Samsung's security framework.

categories.label: Samsung Misc System Apps
categories.reason: Miscellaneous Samsung system apps including data creation tools, quick tools, app linker, and APEX manager. These support various system functions and are generally harmless to keep.
